Pre-order now with Amazon price guarantee: https://t.co/fwEcxllGml from @ManningBooks 

You will learn how to:

• Define clear business objectives for digital twins
• Create digital representations of physical systems
• Blend computer vision, OCR, and generative AI with 3D geometric models

• Represent real-world systems as knowledge graphs
• Machine learning and agentic AI for analysis and decision-making

user.name: Thomas Wolf
user.description: Co-founder at @HuggingFace - moonshots - angel

fullText:
Happy Birthday, America.

Ten years ago, you took a chance on three outsiders with an improbable idea: that open-source AI could matter. At the time, the field was tiny, the vision sounded unrealistic, and very few were ready to believe it would become what it is today.

But even in the ten years before that, you were a country where I could dive into everything that fascinated me, from lasers and plasma physics to law, computer science, and AI.

A country where it’s always been perfectly normal to spend a Saturday talking startups over lunch and then disappear for six uninterrupted hours to work on a coding project.

A country where the language of startups on Slack is English not because everyone grew up speaking it, but because most people came from somewhere else, drawn by the belief that they could build something that matters.

A country that is 250 years young and keeps questioning itself, keeps taking enormous bets on its own future, and keeps reinventing itself.

I’m grateful to be living through America’s first quarter millennium.

Happy birthday, America 🇺🇸

fullText:
Say hello to Omnigent 👋

Databricks' open-source meta-harness helps developers orchestrate AI coding workflows across multiple coding agents, making it easy to use the right model for each task.

In this video, @cole_medin explains why meta-harnesses are becoming an important part of modern AI coding and demonstrates how Omnigent orchestrates coding assistants with shared sessions, guardrails, and human-in-the-loop workflows.
https://t.co/C4lAcXwNUA

fullText:
Thinking Analytically — A Guide for Making Data-Driven Decisions: https://t.co/NUlFJLLnLR

Amazon summary:

This book introduces the ‘Thinking Analytically’ pyramid, covering:
1. Cognitive Foundation: Understand how cognitive limitations impact data interpretation.
2. Probability Mindset: Improve probabilistic thinking and avoid common errors.
3. Data Quality: Learn how subtle problems can distort the results.
4. Experimental Design: Explore how context affects the conclusions you can draw.
5. Analytics: Produce sound results by integrating all pyramid levels.
